welcome.label: // my-project
welcome.title:
Explore a consumer project
that uses the plugin.
welcome.subtitle: This simulated application enables vision-plugin from .claude/settings.json. The plugin package itself stays separate; switch to vision-plugin to inspect its shared files.
welcome.secondary: Start with the project files to see what lives in the repo, then open the plugin view to inspect the reusable package that powers reviews and Zeplin.

claude-md.title: Project Memory
claude-md.description: App-specific context that stays in the consuming repository.

settings.title: Project Settings
settings.description: Claude settings that enable the shared plugin for this repo.

code.title: Application Code
code.description: Normal project files that remain in the repo even when shared Claude tooling comes from a plugin.

plugin.title: Plugin Usage
plugin.description: How this project depends on the external vision-plugin package.

terminal.welcomeTagline: Inspect the consumer project first, then switch to vision-plugin to see the shared package behind it.

quickStart.description: verify the project and plugin setup
see how Claude would review project changes

howToExplore: Open .claude/settings.json to see vision-plugin enabled for the repo
Read CLAUDE.md for project-specific rules that do not belong in the plugin
Open src/ to see where the real application code still lives
Switch to vision-plugin to inspect the shared package itself

doctorChecks.label: CLAUDE.md
doctorChecks.detail: workspace memory loaded from project root

doctorChecks.label: External context
doctorChecks.detail: Zeplin comes from the enabled plugin, not from the repo root

initConventions: App-specific code and rules stay in the repo
Shared Claude automation comes from vision-plugin
Plugin internals are explored in the separate vision-plugin view
memoryEntries: Zeplin is provided by the plugin, so the project does not need its own .mcp.json by default
CLAUDE.md stays project-specific even when reviews and MCP come from a shared plugin
Application code still lives in src/, outside the plugin package
